Linear-Coding-Agent (188 stars): Two-agent pattern (Initializer + Coding Agents) with state in Linear. Initializer reads spec→creates 50 issues; agents query→claim→implement→Puppeteer test→Done. Components: autonomous_agent_demo.py, agent.py, client.py (Claude SDK+MCP), security.py (bash allowlist). MCP: Linear (24 tools)+Puppeteer (7 tools). Security: 3 layers (sandbox, filesystem, 15-cmd allowlist). Cost: $8-19/user/mo. YOUR-CLAUDE recommendation: Option B Hybrid (3-5 sessions): /pr skill, optional Linear sync, Puppeteer MCP, /visual-test skill, integration tests.

Agent orchestration patterns (session 163): 5 core patterns: Sequential (DONE), Concurrent (NOT DONE—30-40% latency gain), Handoff (PARTIAL), Group Chat (NOT DONE), Magentic (PARTIAL). Quick wins: (1) Consensus before remember_this (1-2 days), (2) Context compaction (3-5 days), (3) Anomaly detection (5-7 days). Protocols: MCP (aligned), A2A by Google (150+ orgs, NOT DONE), ACP/ANP (emerging). Task routing: capability-based registry recommended (O(n)). Benchmarks: 1445% inquiry surge, 45% faster resolution, 60% more accurate.

Task decomposition research (arxiv): TDAG (2402.10178)—dynamically decomposes→generates subagents. DynTaskMAS (2503.07675)—dynamic task graph+async execution. Modular (2511.01149)—LLM hierarchical decomposition. Learning (2502.13376)—optimal decomposition from environment. Pattern for Torus: use dependency graph→determine execution order before spawning agents; async parallel where dependencies allow (already in torus-wave.py).

Proposed tool: get_teammate_context(agent_name, max_tokens=300)—reads teammate JSONL, extracts last 5 actions+outcomes, ~200-300 tokens. Benefits: real-time freshness, duplicate work prevention, better coordination. Drawbacks: file lock failures, noise from reasoning, session-only. Implementation: ~120 lines, ~15 tests, zero existing risk. Verdict: worth building but low priority—breaks even after preventing 1 duplicate per session. Best for 3+ teams with dependent tasks.

CLAUDE.MD updated (2026-02-11): Replaced rigid "teams for 3+ steps" with nuanced AGENT DELEGATION GUIDE. Key changes: (1) Rule 3: sub-agents for independent/parallel; teams for 6+ dependent steps, (2) Rule 4: both sub-agents+teams, (3) Replaced TEAM WORKFLOW with AGENT DELEGATION GUIDE. Decision table: 2-5 independent→sub-agents, 2-5 dependent→sub-agents, 5-7→either, 7+→teams, cross-session→sub-agents+memory. Rationale: Memory MCP shifts team threshold 3→6-7.

GitHub sync rules (session 375): SCRUB before syncing: (1) ARCHITECTURE.md—remove references, (2) USAGE_GUIDE.md—remove references, (3) Hardcoded paths→dynamic os.path.expanduser, (4) Session numbers in commits—omit, (5-7) .experience_archive.csv, .circuit_breaker.json, .tool_fingerprints.json—exclude/reset. SAFE: .claudeignore, CLAUDE.md, settings.json, hooks/gates/*.py, skills/. DO NOT: docs/diagrams/html/, hook files. Always scrub EVERY file, not just diffs. Use explicit git add, never git add -A.

Framework comparison expanded (session 145): Added Torus GitHub (72) vs Torus Local (87)—15-point gap from bugs. Added GOTCHA/ATLAS (61)—6-layer agentic framework by Mansel Scheffel (Goals, Orchestration, Tools, Context, Hard prompts, Args). Philosophy: push reliability→deterministic tools, reasoning→LLM. File-based memory, no mechanical gates. Score: Token 70, Cost 65, Speed 80, Consistency 60, Learning 40, Quality 70, Memory 35, Reliability 65. Different target: business automation vs software dev.

Toroidal Teams Phase 3 complete (session 402, branch Toroidal-teams). 7 tasks: (1) Gate 13 co-claim: COCLAIMS_FILE+_read_coclaims(), checks .file_coclaims.json, (2) Watcher: reads shared_files, writes coclaims 1hr expiry, (3) sessions.json+session_register.sh: ~./toroidal/sessions.json with role/session_id/model/status, (4) session_capture_hook.py: SessionStart hook updates sessions.json with real ID, (5) teardown.sh: kills+registers stopped, (6) manage.sh: status/list/compact/clear/send/suspend/resume. File-based IPC, inotifywait watcher, shared LanceDB, co-claims for safe file access.

Torus Swarm pattern (session 368): main Claude = orchestrator. (1) Task decomposition→/tmp/agentN-task.md, (2) Launch: tmux new-session→claude --dangerously-skip-permissions, (3) Monitor: tmux capture-pane, (4) Steer: tmux send-keys, (5) Verify: git status/builds. Lessons: NO -p (buffers output), NO inline prompts (use files), check tmux session, shared directory>worktrees, Memory MCP=coordination bus, Gates enforce discipline, practical limit 3-5 agents. Parallel: Agent 2 genesis~8min, Agent 1 op-geth~8min.

Torus-web deep-read (2026-02-11): 16 libs, 12 models, 40+ endpoints, 17 pages, 30+ components. Flow: wallet→POST /api/users; Steam OpenID→verification; cron collects stats 60min; rankings hourly→top 20% rewards; identity verify→bonus/referral; cache 24h TTL. Algorithms: reward=(pool×userPoints/total), game=(value×multiplier×boosts), rate-limit=token bucket (200 req/min). Auth: JWT+CSRF+Steam state. NOT built: TRS transfers (DB only), social/DeFi/WebSockets.

6 actionable patterns for Torus (session268): (1) Dynamic Topology Selection (AdaptOrch)—task graph→select topology 12-23% gain, (2) Explorer/Worker/Validator Triad (SwarmSys)—maps to researcher/builder/stress-tester, (3) SOPs (MetaGPT)—formalize verification checkpoints, (4) Async Task Graph (DynTaskMAS)—true async, unblock on dependency completion, (5) Comms Penalty (Agent-GSPO)—penalize excessive messages, (6) Population Evolution (SwarmAgentic)—PSO-inspired skill/gate selection. Most relevant: AdaptOrch, DynTaskMAS, SwarmSys, MetaGPT.

AgentEvolver (arXiv:2511.10395): Three mechanisms: (1) Self-Questioning—curiosity-driven tasks reduce handcrafted data, (2) Self-Navigating—improves exploration via experience reuse+hybrid policy, (3) Self-Attributing—contribution-weighted rewards not uniform. Torus applicability: Self-Attributing=causal chain tracking (different events have different weight), Self-Questioning=gate-generated tasks, Experience reuse=memory-first. Pattern: enhance causal chain with contribution_weight field. Backpropagate credit to record_attempt() steps—surfaces which steps matter.

MetaAgent (arXiv:2508.00271): Learning-by-doing—expertise via practice. Gaps→natural language help→best tool. Self-reflects+verifies post-task, distills to actionable text, persistent knowledge base. No parameter updates. Torus applicability: gates already verify claims; missing auto-distillation from gate fires. Pattern: after successful fix verified by Bash, auto-call remember_this() with "Fix for [error]: [strategy]. Verified by: [test hash]." Mirrors post-task reflection without manual effort.

ZeroBrain extension integration (2026-03-09): Created 5 extensions at /home/crab/projects/zerobrain/zerobrain/extensions/: (1) tool_gate.py—12 gates, Enforcer singleton, blocks via closure, (2) loop_monitor.py—loop_start_time+iteration, (3) monologue_reset.py—resets per user turn, (4) test_tracker.py—matches 11 patterns, updates test state, (5) output_scanner.py—8 injection patterns, prepends warning. memory/adapter.py—MemoryQualityAdapter (dedup MD5+similarity, quality search=similarity×recency). All imports OK, tested.
